threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think waiting for the bus means it’ll arrive in 10 mins, not ‘whenever it feels like it’, right? Classic newbie mistake.
Last night on the N68, a bloke serenaded the bus with old reggae tunes. By the time we hit Clapham, half the passengers were singing along.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids using a bus shelter as a stage for their breakdance battle, I felt like Neukölln had welcomed me home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a Turkish bakery open 24/7, serving fresh simit and çay while the rest of the city sleeps.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot locals by their indifference to the endless BART delays. If they roll their eyes at the mention of a broken train, they’ve been here a while.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy serenade commuters on the 47 with a ukulele while the Muni app crashed. Only in SF do you get free entertainment and chaos in one ride.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You’d think the plaça is just for selfies, but it's where locals catch up in the evening, arguing over Barça's last match while kids play tag.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always order an 'espressito' at the café. No local would add that '-ito' suffix. It's a surefire giveaway.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver says 'no change'. Everyone else just nods and either arrives or walks away.
The 59 bus driver last night blasted some old DnB on the speakers—had the whole bus humming along. Never thought I’d have a dance party at 2 AM!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids commandeering an empty tram stop for a makeshift soccer game, I realized this place has its own rhythm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how the U-Bahn stations here have graffiti that tells stories? Each one feels like a mini gallery, reflecting the city's spirit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know not to trust the bus schedule at 4 PM on Fridays—it’s a game of luck, not logistics.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo try to get on the bus with a giant inflatable flamingo. Muni drivers didn’t even blink. Just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to Parc Güell, but miss the magic of Plaça del Sol at dusk—locals gather, sharing stories and laughter as the sun dips.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their struggle to pass the 'caganer' statue at Christmas without a double-take—locals just roll their eyes.
